info.title: Mailtarget Dashboard API
info.description:
At the core of Mailtarget's services lie our powerful APIs, designed to give developers around the globe an easy and efficient means to manage email communications. Whether you need to send, receive, or track emails, our APIs provide the tools you need to integrate seamlessly into your applications. To acquaint you with the capabilities of Mailtarget's APIs, here are essential insights to guide you:

## Endpoint

The Mailtarget API adheres to REST principles, ensuring a consistent and predictable interface. Authenticated users can engage with our various URIs using the designated HTTP request methods. To guarantee security, all requests must be made over HTTPS, enforcing TLS encryption throughout.

`https://apiconfig.mailtarget.co/v1`

If you have an Enterprise account with a dedicated endpoint, please reach out to your account manager for further details.

## Authentication

All API requests must be authenticated using a **Mailtarget API Key**.

Include your API key in the `Authorization` request header using the **Bearer** scheme:

```curl
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

**Example request:**

```curl
curl --location --request GET 'https://apiconfig.mailtarget.co/v1/account' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'
```

> **Security note:** Keep your API keys in a secure location and never expose them in client-side code. If your key is compromised, regenerate it immediately from the [Mailtarget dashboard](https://docs.mtarget.co/en/articles/10382870-api-key).

### API Conventions

* Trailing slashes are ignored: `/account` is equivalent to `/account/`.
* URL paths, query parameter names, and JSON field names are **case sensitive**.
* URL paths use **lowercase with dashes** separating words.
* Query parameters and JSON fields use **camelCase**.
* HTTP status codes indicate success or failure; additional details are returned in the response body.
* All APIs return standard HTTP error code formats.
* Unexpected query parameters and request body fields are ignored.
* Our JSON number type is bounded to a signed 32-bit integer.

## Errors

When making an API call, it is possible to receive an error message in response. This error message can indicate either an issue with your request or a problem that occurred on our side. Errors are accompanied by an error code and JSON data that provides a more specific message, description, and API code.

```json
422 Unprocessable Entity
{
  "errors": [
    {
      "message": "required field is missing",
      "description": "content object or template_id required",
      "code": "1400"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Error table

| **CODE** | **STATUS NAME**          | **DESCRIPTION**                                                                | **SUGGESTED ACTION**                                                                                                                                     |
| -------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 400      | Bad Request              | There is a problem with your request.                                          | Check your request follows the API documentation and uses correct syntax.                                                                                |
| 401      | Unauthorized             | You don't have the needed authorization to make the request.                   | Make sure you are using a valid API key with the necessary permissions for your request.                                                                 |
| 403      | Forbidden                | The server understood the request but refused to fulfill it.                   | See if your Mailtarget plan includes the resource you are requesting and your API key has the necessary permissions.                                     |
| 404      | Not Found                | The server couldn't find the requested file.                                   | Change your request URL to match a valid API endpoint.                                                                                                   |
| 405      | Method Not Allowed       | The resource does not have the specified method. (e.g. PUT on transmissions)   | Change the method to follow the documentation for the resource.                                                                                          |
| 409      | Conflict                 | A conflict arose from your request. (e.g. user already exists with that email) | Modify the payload to clear the conflict.                                                                                                                |
| 413      | Request Entity Too Large | The size of the request exceeds the limit.                                     | Check the documented limit for the API and reduce the size of the request.                                                                               |
| 415      | Unsupported Media Type   | The request is not in a supported format.                                      | Check that your Content-Type header is a supported type and that your request adheres to the documentation.                                              |
| 420      | Exceed Sending Limit     | You exceeded your daily, monthly, or sandbox sending limit.                    | Check that you are within the limits of your Mailtarget plan. If you are using the sandbox domain, then you'll need to add a sending domain to continue. |
| 422      | Unprocessable Entity     | The request was syntactically correct but failed due to semantic errors.       | Make sure that your request includes all of the required fields and your data is valid.                                                                  |
| 429      | Too Many Requests        | You sent too many requests in a given time period.                             | Wait 1 to 5 seconds before making another request. Heavier usage patterns may require a more sophisticated and incremental backoff strategy.             |
| 500      | Internal Server Error    | Something went wrong on our end.                                               | Try the request again later. If the error does not resolve, check for service updates. You can also contact Support if your issue persists.              |
| 503      | Service Unavailable      | We are experiencing higher than normal levels of traffic.                      | Try the request again later.                                                                                                                             |

### Environment Setup

Our collection is configured to utilize two Postman variables:

* **API_KEY**: Used for authenticating requests. Assign it a valid API key.
* **BASE_URL**: Set as `https://apiconfig.mailtarget.co/v1`.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Endpoints for retrieving account information and email sending usage quota. Use these endpoints to verify account configuration, monitor sending volume, and enforce quota limits before initiating sends.

tags.name: Suppression
tags.description: Endpoints for managing the suppression list — email addresses that should not receive emails due to bounces, spam complaints, or unsubscribe requests. Use these endpoints to protect sender reputation and comply with suppression policies.

tags.name: Sending Domains
tags.description:
The **Sending Domains API** allows users to manage their sending domains to ensure proper email authentication and deliverability. The API provides functionalities for **creating, retrieving, deleting, and verifying** sending domains.

## Core Functions

1. **Create Sending Domain** – Register a new sending domain.
2. **Get List of Sending Domains** – Retrieve all registered sending domains with pagination.
3. **Delete Sending Domain** – Remove a sending domain from the system.
4. **Verify Sending Domain** – Check and confirm if a sending domain has been authenticated via DNS records.

By integrating this API, users can automate domain registration, verification, and management for email-sending purposes.

## Limitation

* **Rate Limits:** API calls are limited to **X requests per minute** to prevent abuse.
* **Authentication Required:** All API requests must include a valid **API key** or **OAuth token**.
* **Domain Verification Process:** Newly added domains remain in a **pending state** until users complete DNS authentication.
* **SPF & DKIM Requirements:** Sending domains must have valid **SPF and DKIM** records configured before they can be used.
* **Deletion Restriction:** A sending domain **cannot be deleted** if it is actively being used in ongoing email campaigns.

## Use Case

* **Automated Domain Registration & Verification:** Users can register a sending domain and retrieve the necessary authentication records (SPF & DKIM) for DNS configuration.
* **Domain Status Monitoring:** Businesses can track the verification status of their sending domains and ensure compliance with authentication standards.
* **Programmatic Cleanup of Inactive Domains:** Unused or unverified domains can be automatically removed from the system to maintain a clean and secure email environment.
* **Security & Compliance:** The verification process helps enforce proper email authentication, reducing the risk of email spoofing and phishing attacks.

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description:
The API Key API allows users to manage their API keys, including creating, retrieving, updating, and deleting keys. This API is essential for authentication and authorization of external integrations with the platform.

## Limitation

* API keys must be kept confidential and should not be shared or exposed.
* Some API key permissions may be restricted based on user roles and account type.
* Deleting an API key immediately revokes access for any service using it.

## Use Case

1. **Integration Authentication** – A developer generates an API key to authenticate a third-party system with the platform.
2. **Security Management** – An admin retrieves a list of API keys to audit and revoke unused keys.
3. **Key Rotation** – A user updates an existing API key to maintain security without disrupting existing integrations.

tags.name: Templates
tags.description:
The Template API allows users to create and retrieve all email templates via API. This enables seamless integration with email automation systems by storing and managing reusable templates.

Users can send emails through the API or SMTP by utilizing a template ID along with specific content parameters or shortcodes. This approach eliminates the need to send complete string or HTML formats during email transmission, allowing for more efficient and streamlined email sending processes.

### Example Workflow:

1. **Create a Template:** Define a template and assign it a unique template ID.
2. **Prepare Content Parameters:** Determine the shortcodes and parameters needed by the template, such as `{name}`, `{date}`, `{company}`, etc.
3. **Send Email via API:**
   * Specify the template ID when preparing the email transmission.
   * Include only the necessary content parameters relevant to the particular sending instance.

### Key Features

* **Template ID:** Use the template ID to identify which template to use for the email message. This ID corresponds to a template stored and managed within your system.
* **Content Parameters/Shortcodes:** Define content or shortcodes that are used within the template. These parameters are placeholders in the template that will be dynamically replaced with real data at the time of sending.

## Limitation

* The maximum number of templates a user can create may be subject to account-level restrictions.
* Templates must adhere to the supported email format and size limits.
* Some template attributes may be required for proper rendering in email clients.

## Use Case

1. **Email Automation** – A user wants to create predefined email templates to send transactional emails programmatically.
2. **Template Management** – A system fetches all available templates to display in a user interface for selection.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: Endpoints for retrieving email transmission analytics, delivery status, event lists, and aggregated performance summaries. Use these endpoints to monitor sending activity, track transmission results, and analyze email delivery performance.

get.description:
Provides **aggregated analytics data** for email sending activity within a specified date range.

This endpoint summarizes delivery and engagement performance metrics to help developers
and system administrators **monitor overall email performance**.

**Use cases:**
- Build custom analytics dashboards
- Monitor sending performance
- Detect anomalies such as spikes in bounce or complaint rates
- Optimize email sending strategies

parameters.name: sandbox
parameters.description: Enable sandbox mode to retrieve sandbox data

200.description: Aggregated email delivery and engagement metrics for the specified period

400.description: Bad request - Invalid date format or range

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Returns **aggregated analytics data broken down by a specific dimension** within a date range.

Supported groupBy dimensions: `DAY`, `SENDING_DOMAIN`, `RECIPIENT_DOMAIN`, `SUBACCOUNT_ID`, `IP_POOL`, `LABEL`.
Each result entry includes the same delivery and engagement metrics as the summary endpoint,
plus a `name` field representing the value of the grouped dimension.

**Use cases:**
- Analyze performance per sending domain or IP pool
- Compare engagement metrics across sub-accounts
- Identify day-by-day trends in delivery or bounce rates

parameters.name: sandbox
parameters.description: Enable sandbox mode to retrieve sandbox data

200.description: List of analytics summaries grouped by the specified dimension

400.description: Bad request - Invalid date format, range, or groupBy value

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Returns the **status and statistics of a specific email transmission** identified by its transmissionId.

A transmission represents a sending request that may contain one or more recipients.
This endpoint provides delivery results and engagement metrics (delivered, opened, clicked, bounced, unsubscribed)
associated with that transmission.

**Use cases:**
- Tracking delivery performance of a sent email
- Monitoring transactional email status
- Building internal reporting tools
- Verifying if a message was successfully delivered

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the email transmission

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Returns a **list of events related to a specific transmissionId**.

Events may include email injection, delivery, opens, link clicks, bounces, or other interaction events.
This endpoint is particularly useful when applications cannot receive webhook notifications
and need to **poll for event updates**.

**Use cases:**
- Pulling delivery and engagement events
- Building custom analytics dashboards
- Debugging email delivery or engagement issues

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the email transmission

200.description: List of transmission events (injection, delivery, open, click, bounce)

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Returns **basic metadata about the authenticated account**, including company details, account status, and default configuration.

This endpoint can be used to **verify the account configuration** before sending emails.

**Use cases:**
- Verify account status before initiating a send
- Display account details in dashboards
- Confirm default sending domain configuration
- Retrieve timezone settings for analytics alignment

200.description: Account information including company details, plan, status, default sending domain, and timezone

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Returns a summary of the **email sending usage, sending limits, and operational constraints** configured for the account.

This endpoint provides insights into how many emails have been sent, how much quota remains, percentage of quota used, rate limits, and maximum transmission constraints.

This endpoint is useful for applications that need to **monitor sending activity and prevent exceeding the quota**.

**Use cases:**
- Monitor email sending volume
- Prevent sending when the account quota is close to being exceeded
- Automatically throttle sending applications when limits are approaching
- Split large sending jobs into smaller batches to comply with rate limits
- Prevent sending requests that exceed the maximum recipients per transmission

200.description: Account usage statistics including emails sent, quota remaining, rate limits, and transmission constraints

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Retrieves all API keys associated with a user's account.
API keys are essential for authenticating and authorizing API requests,
this endpoint allows users to view active keys and their permissions.

post.description:
Creates a new API key that can be used for authentication when interacting with the SMTP or API.
Users can generate multiple API keys for different integrations,
enabling better security and management of access control.

get.description: Fetches details of a specific API key based on its unique identifier.

put.description:
Updates the details of an existing API key, including modifying its permissions or extending its validity.
This endpoint is useful for maintaining security while ensuring uninterrupted access to API integrations.

delete.description:
Removes an API key, revoking access for any applications or services using it.
Deleting unused or compromised API keys helps prevent unauthorized access and enhances security.

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of registered sending domains.
This endpoint allows users to fetch all sending domains associated with their account,
including details such as domain name, verification status, and authentication settings.
Pagination ensures efficient data retrieval when managing a large number of domains.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page, start from 1 until n length of pages

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of data to return per page

post.description:
Registers a new sending domain and returns authentication details.
When adding a new sending domain, users receive necessary DNS records (SPF, DKIM, and DMARC) that must be configured to authenticate emails.
Proper configuration of these records improves email deliverability and ensures compliance with email authentication standards.

get.description:
Fetches a list of sending domains that have incomplete or missing SPF authentication.
This endpoint helps users identify domains that require SPF record updates to prevent email spoofing and improve sender reputation.

Retrieves details of a specific sending domain based on its unique identifier
Users can check the domain's verification status, authentication records,
and configuration settings to ensure proper email delivery setup.

delete.description:
Removes a sending domain from the system
Once deleted, the domain can no longer be used for sending emails,
and its associated authentication records become invalid.
This action is irreversible, so users should confirm before proceeding.

put.description:
Enables sharing of a sending domain with other users or teams within the same platform.
This functionality is useful for organizations that manage multiple accounts
and need to grant controlled access to a verified domain.

get.description:
Provides SPF record recommendations for a specific sending domain.
This helps users correctly configure their SPF records to authorize email senders
and avoid deliverability issues due to authentication failures.

put.description:
Checks whether the TXT records required for domain authentication
have been correctly set up in the domain's DNS.
This verification process ensures that the domain is properly authenticated for sending emails.

get.description: Retrieves all Sub Accounts associated with a user's account.

post.description: Creates a new Sub Account associated with a user's account.

get.description: Retrieves a specific Sub Account associated with a user's account.

put.description: Updates a specific Sub Account associated with a user's account.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of **suppressed email addresses** that should not receive emails due to delivery failures, spam complaints, or unsubscribe requests.

Suppression lists help **protect sender reputation** by preventing emails from being sent to problematic addresses.

Suppression types include:
- `bounce` — Address that previously hard bounced
- `spam_complaint` — Recipient marked the email as spam
- `unsubscribe` — Recipient requested to unsubscribe

200.description: Suppression list with total count and list of suppressed email entries

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

get.description:
Retrieves all email templates created by the user.
Templates help streamline email campaigns by allowing users to store
and reuse pre-designed email content, ensuring consistency in branding and messaging.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page, start from 1 until n length of pages

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of data to return per page

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Search keyword on data in templates

post.description: Create a new email template that can be used in transactional messages.

BearerAuth.description: Enter your Mailtarget API key with the **Bearer** prefix, e.g. `Bearer YOUR_API_KEY`
BearerAuth.name: Authorization
